---
pagetitle: "Start with a Design-Loop Card - Architecture 2.0"
description: "Complete, validate, and review an Architecture 2.0 design-loop card in 30 minutes."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<main class="site-wrap start-page">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">30-minute workflow</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero start-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Start with the core artifact</div>
  <h1>Complete one design-loop card</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">
    Bound a claim, expose its evidence, and record what can reject it before a
    design decision moves forward.
  </p>
  <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Design-loop card downloads">
    <a class="site-action" href="/design-loop-card/template.yaml" download>Download YAML <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&darr;</span></a>
    <a class="site-action secondary" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/design-loop-card/template.md" download>Download Markdown <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&darr;</span></a>
    <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/book/appendices/appendix-b-design-loop-card/">Read Appendix B</a>
  </nav>
</section>

<section class="start-workflow" aria-labelledby="workflow-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="workflow-title">Fill, validate, review</h2>
    <p>Use Level 0 for context, Level 1 for an auditable ledger, Level 2 for replay, and Level 3 when an independent gate can reject the result.</p>
  </div>

  <ol class="start-step-list">
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">01</span>
        <div><p>10 minutes</p><h3>Fill the card</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Name the bounded objective, legal design choices, representation, environment, method role, feedback cost, evidence, failed alternatives, rejection path, commitment boundary, and human owner.</p>
      <p class="start-step-note">Put a paper's exact claim and non-claim under <code>intent.claim_boundary</code>. It is a view of intent, not a thirteenth field.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">02</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Validate the record</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Run the repository validator against the claimed conformance level. Fix every reported path before review.</p>
      <pre class="start-command"><code>git clone https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2.git
cd arch2
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
./arch2 validate card my-card.yaml</code></pre>
      <p class="start-step-note">Run these commands from a repository clone. The validator needs Python only; the heavier book toolchain is not required. See <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#building-the-site-locally">local project setup</a> for the rest of the site.</p>
      <p class="start-step-note">The validator rejects unsupported schema versions, unknown fields, missing level requirements, and a dependent Level 3 rejection gate.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">03</span>
        <div><p>15 minutes</p><h3>Review the commitment</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Give the card and its evidence pointers to someone who did not produce the result. Record the disposition and the evidence that would change it.</p>
      <ul class="start-review-list">
        <li>Is the task bounded enough to evaluate?</li>
        <li>Does the representation support every allowed action?</li>
        <li>Is the feedback budget realistic?</li>
        <li>Does the evidence match the commitment?</li>
        <li>Can an identified gate reject the result?</li>
      </ul>
    </li>
  </ol>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="artifacts-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="artifacts-title">Working artifacts</h2>
    <p>The schema is the source of truth. The examples show the cumulative requirements without inventing additional card fields.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/schemas/design-loop-card.v1.schema.json">
      <span class="site-pill">Contract</span>
      <strong>JSON Schema v1.0</strong>
      <span>Machine-readable field and conformance rules</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/level-3-independent.yaml">
      <span class="site-pill hot">Completed</span>
      <strong>Level 3 example</strong>
      <span>Independent review of a bounded accelerator study</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/schemas/design-loop-card.md">
      <span class="site-pill">Compatibility</span>
      <strong>Version policy</strong>
      <span>Extension, compatibility, and change rules</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/examples/design-loop-cards">
      <span class="site-pill">Examples</span>
      <strong>Levels 0 through 3</strong>
      <span>One valid record for each conformance level</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section instructor-exercise" aria-labelledby="exercise-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="exercise-title">Instructor exercise</h2>
    <p>Pairs can complete this paper-to-loop review in one 30-minute block.</p>
  </div>
  <ol>
    <li><strong>Choose, 2 minutes.</strong> Select one paper with a concrete architecture result.</li>
    <li><strong>Extract, 8 minutes.</strong> One student fills intent through feedback budget. The other fills evidence through human decision.</li>
    <li><strong>Validate, 5 minutes.</strong> Agree on a conformance level and run the validator. A missing required field caps the claim.</li>
    <li><strong>Cross-review, 10 minutes.</strong> Exchange cards with another pair and apply the five review questions.</li>
    <li><strong>Debrief, 5 minutes.</strong> Report one hidden assumption, one negative trace that should exist, and the strongest commitment the evidence supports.</li>
  </ol>
</section>
</main>
```
